subject: Science
chapter: Nature of Matter: Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

key_points: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space
A mixture is a combination of two or more substances that are not chemically combined
Pure substances consist of only one type of particle
Elements are pure substances made of only one type of atom
Compounds are formed when two or more elements chemically combine in fixed proportions
examples: Air is a mixture of gases such as nitrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide
Water is a compound made of hydrogen and oxygen
Iron is an example of an element
Bronze is an alloy made from copper and tin
Quartz is a naturally occurring mineral
formulas_or_processes: Electrolysis of water: Water → Hydrogen + Oxygen
Chemical reaction example: Iron + Sulfur → Iron sulfide
Limewater test: Carbon dioxide + Limewater → Calcium carbonate (milky appearance)
Separation of mixtures: Filtration, evaporation, and magnetic separation
exam_tips: Understand the difference between mixtures, elements, and compounds
Remember that elements contain only one type of atom
Know examples of alloys such as brass and bronze
Revise important experiments such as electrolysis of water and the iron-sulfur reaction
